media.base_medium: MDC = anoxic minimal M9-derived coculture medium of LaSarre et al. 2017 (ISME J 11:337-348), re-used via Sangani et al. 2019 (FEMS Microbiol Lett 366:fnz162; ref 63 of this paper). This paper adds a MOPS buffer (100 or 200 mM, pH 7.0) and prepares it anoxically under N2. Trace-element solution is that of Kremer et al. 2015 (PNAS 112:2222-2226).

sources_chased.what: target paper methods (PMC12997748 / bioRxiv 2025.08.29.673078 / AEM aem.01745-25): MDC supplements, MOPS buffer, N2 anoxic prep
ref 63 = Sangani, McCully, LaSarre, McKinlay 2019, FEMS Microbiol Lett 366:fnz162, doi:10.1093/femsle/fnz162 (source cited for MDC)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch doi.org -> academic.oup.com; confirms MDC and points to LaSarre 2017 as the full recipe source

sources_chased.what: MDC full base recipe = LaSarre, Deutschbauer, Love, McKinlay 2017, ISME J 11:337-348, doi:10.1038/ismej.2016.141 (Nature paywalled; OUP mirror open)
trace-element solution = Kremer, LaSarre, Posto, McKinlay 2015, PNAS 112:2222-2226, doi:10.1073/pnas.1420663112; full composition (incl. 'Metals 44') reproduced in same-group patent WO2016109286A1
sources_chased.how: PNAS 403; recipe recovered verbatim from Google Patents WO2016109286A1 (same authors, identical defined medium and trace solution)
added: Metals 44: ZnSO4, MnSO4, CuSO4, Co(NO3)2, Na2B4O7, EDTA, FeSO4

media.notes: Anoxic defined minimal medium (N2 headspace, sealed serum vials/tubes). The current model list captured Ca, Cl, Co, Cu, Fe, K, Mg, Mn, Na, NH4, Pi, SO4, Zn and MOPS but was missing molybdate (Mo) and borate (B) - both present in the Kremer 2015 trace solution - plus thiosulfate, the PABA vitamin, and the N2 gas phase. Bulk phosphate is Na2HPO4 42.5 mM + KH2PO4 22 mM (M9-derived, from LaSarre 2017); sulfate comes chiefly from the 1 mM MgSO4 supplement. Trace-metal salts sit in a 0.1% v/v solution (and 'Metals 44' a further 0.1% v/v within it), so final metal concentrations are extremely low; stock concentrations are reported and conc_mM left null to avoid over-precise double-dilution figures. No nickel and no separate biotin/thiamine/B12 in this defined medium (only PABA).

media.base_medium: Identical to MDC (LaSarre 2017 M9-derived anoxic coculture medium via Sangani 2019 ref 63; trace elements from Kremer 2015), with an additional 80 mM NaCl to raise salinity for Vibrio natriegens.

sources_chased.what: target paper methods (PMC12997748): MDC80 = MDC + 80 mM NaCl; supplements; MOPS; N2 anoxic prep
MDC base recipe chain: ref 63 Sangani 2019 (FEMS Microbiol Lett 366:fnz162) -> LaSarre 2017 (ISME J 11:337-348) -> Kremer 2015 trace solution (PNAS 112:2222-2226) / patent WO2016109286A1
sources_chased.how: WebFetch academic.oup.com (Sangani, LaSarre) + Google Patents WO2016109286A1 (Kremer trace solution + 'Metals 44')
added: trace-element solution: Fe, Mo, Zn, Mn, Cu, Co, B, NTA, EDTA

missing_before: the 80 mM NaCl (defining feature of MDC80)
media.notes: MDC80 is exactly MDC plus 80 mM NaCl (for Vibrio natriegens' halophilic salt requirement). Same recovered additions as MDC: molybdate and borate (from the Kremer 2015 trace solution) plus thiosulfate, PABA vitamin, and the N2 gas phase were absent from the current model list. The extra NaCl adds substantial Na+/Cl- beyond the already-present Na and Cl exchanges. Trace-metal final concentrations are very low (0.1% v/v solution, with 'Metals 44' a further 0.1% v/v); stock concentrations reported, conc_mM left null.
